articles.title: Suvendu Adhikari's PA murder: BJP leader says aide's killing 'pre-planned'
articles.description: West Bengal BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari has called the killing of his personal assistant a "cold-blooded and pre-planned murder." He urged party workers to maintain peace while assuring that the culprits will be brought to justice. Union Minister Sukanta Majumdar echoed these sentiments, stating that the police investigation is progressing and those responsible will not be spared.

articles.title: Suvendu Adhikari calls aide’s killing ‘pre-planned’: 'Result of 15 years of Mahajungle Raj'
articles.description: Condemning the incident, Adhikari told reporters that union minister Sukanta Majumdar had spoken to the deceased’s family

articles.description: The BJP appointed Union Home Minister Amit Shah as the central observer for the election of the leader of the Legislature Party. Odisha Chief Minister Mohan Charan Majhi has been named co-observer. Shah and Majhi are likely to arrive in the Bengal in the coming days.

articles.description: Vijay’s TVK shattered a 50-year-old Dravidian duopoly to emerge as the single-largest party in assembly elections earlier this week, winning 108 seats.

articles.title: Donald Trump says deal with Iran possible, claims military decimated
articles.description: US President Donald Trump said a deal with Iran is 'very possible' following positive talks, adding that their military has been decimated. Negotiations are reportedly advancing on a framework for a ceasefire and broader nuclear talks.

articles.title: Iran wants to negotiate, make a deal, says Trump; no nukes priority
articles.description: US President Donald Trump said Iran wants to negotiate a deal on the West Asia crisis. He stressed that preventing Iran from getting a nuclear weapon is a top priority and that the US is assessing whether any agreement would be satisfactory.

articles.description: The US-Israel war on Iran has undermined New Delhi’s delicate balancing of ties in the Middle East, says RSIS’ Sinderpal Singh.

articles.title: Panchkula: Congress alleges over 8,500 duplicate votes in MC electoral roll
articles.description: Rajya Sabha member and All India Congress Committee general secretary Randeep Singh Surjewala claimed that a detailed analysis of the electoral rolls revealed that 8,543 voters are listed twice, resulting in 17,086 duplicate entries across 20 MC wards

articles.title: "India Forgives Nothing": Air Force Posts Video On 1 Year Of Op Sindoor
articles.description: Operation Sindoor Anniversary: "India forgets nothing. India forgives nothing," IAF said in its video.

articles.title: Suvendu Adhikari's assistant shot dead 2 days after BJP's mega Bengal victory
articles.description: In a shocking development, a close aide of West Bengal BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari was shot dead in Madhyamgram area near Kolkata on Wednesday night, triggering tension in the locality and prompting a police investigation.The victim has been identified as Chandra Nath Dutt, personal assistant to Adhikari. According to initial inputs, the incident involved an exchange of fire in which at least four rounds were reportedly fired before Chandra was fatally shot.

articles.description: Amid rising tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, the United States and Iran may be edging closer to a breakthrough deal to prevent another round of war. Reports claim the White House has sent Tehran a one-page memorandum outlining conditions for ending hostilities and setting a framework for nuclear negotiations.

articles.title: Nishant among new ministers likely to take oath today
articles.description: In a noteworthy political shift, Bihar is set to see a cabinet expansion this Thursday, with Nishant Kumar, son of Nitish Kumar, poised to step into a ministerial role. The occasion will be graced by Prime Minister Modi, who will oversee the oath-taking ceremony at the historic Gandhi Maidan.

articles.description: Vijayvargia, a former National General Secretary of the BJP, said the installation of a government guided by “nationalist ideology” in a border state would strengthen internal security and benefit the country.

articles.title: Gram Sabhas that gave nod to Nicobar project did not have mandatory 50% quorum
articles.description: Gram Sabhas approving the Nicobar project lacked the required 50% quorum, with attendance only between 1% and 12%.

articles.title: Tripura CPIM Brainstorms Revival Strategy After Electoral Setbacks
articles.description: CPIM State Committee Meeting Agartala: The Tripura unit of the CPIM began a crucial two-day State Committee meeting here today. Led by State Secretary Jitendra Choudhury, the party is analyzing recent losses in the TTAADC and Dharmanagar polls. The deliberations will focus on the party’s revival strategy after the election debacle in both. articles.title: Israeli court rejects appeal by detained Gaza aid flotilla activists
articles.description: An Israeli court on Wednesday rejected an appeal by two foreign nationals, one Spanish and one Brazilian, challenging their detention in Israel. The two men were seized in international waters last week while participating in a humanitarian aid flotilla seeking to break Israel’s blockade of the Gaza Strip. The ruling means an earlier decision extending their detention remains in place.

articles.title: White House may be planning to bring a law to ensure Anthropic, Google and other companies cannot say 'No' to the US government in future
articles.description: The US government is reportedly exploring new measures to compel AI firms like Google and Anthropic to cooperate on government use of their tools. Proposals include a vetting system for advanced AI models and restrictions on companies refusing government demands, potentially impacting national security applications and raising industry concerns about development speed.

articles.title: US nears consensus with Iran on one-page memorandum to end war
Will Vijay’s Numbers Fall In Line? The Majority Question
articles.description: Vijay, the chief of Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK), has claimed to form the next Tamil Nadu government but lacks the necessary support of 118 MLAs, having won only 108 seats.

articles.title: One Vote, One History: Anand Mahindra Wants This TN Election Math Shown In Every Classroom
articles.description: Anand Mahindra praised the "power of one vote" after TVK's dramatic one-vote win in Tamil Nadu's Tiruppattur constituency.

articles.title: TVK At 112 with Congress Support, Still 6 Short As TN Governor Not Convinced Claim
articles.description: Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam chief Vijay’s bid to form the government in Tamil Nadu faces uncertainty as the party falls short of the majority mark. With 107 seats and support from Congress’ 5 MLAs, TVK’s tally stands at 112, still six short of 118. Congress has extended conditional support, insisting that “communal forces” be kept out. Vijay has written to Governor Rajendra Arlekar seeking time to prove majority. Meanwhile, the VCK is undecided, and Left parties and IUML have declined support, leaving the state in a tense political situation.

articles.title: Move to install statues of leaders from Nehru-Gandhi family questioned
articles.description: Opposition arises against installing Nehru-Gandhi family statues in Kalaburagi, citing lack of transparency and calls for broader representation.

articles.title: Nishant Kumar, son of JD(U) chief Nitish Kumar, likely to join Bihar cabinet under CM Samrat Chaudhary
articles.description: The development comes weeks after the BJP formed the government in Bihar, following Nitish Kumar’s move to the Rajya Sabha.

articles.title: How judicial intervention shapes the doctrine of Essential Religious Practices
articles.description: In the context of the Sabarimala review case, complex constitutional questions are debated. What does the doctrine of the Essential Religious Practices (ERP) say? What is the scope of morality under Article 25(1) of the Constitution?

articles.title: A tale of two CMs: Mamata Banerjee refuses to step down, Himanta Biswa Sarma tenders resignation
articles.description: While the stage is set for the BJP to form governments in both Assam and West Bengal, the political scenes unfolding in the two neighbouring states couldn’t be more different.

articles.title: Can Mamata Banerjee Refuse to Resign? Rules Explained Amid Bengal Power Shift
articles.description: Constitutionally, a Chief Minister is not automatically removed after an election loss and can remain in office until resigning or being dismissed. While democratic convention calls for immediate resignation, the Governor can intervene. The Governor may ask the CM to prove their majority through a floor test in the legislative assembly.

articles.title: Supreme Court collegium approves names of 10 advocates as judges of Punjab and Haryana HC
articles.description: The Supreme Court collegium has approved the appointment of ten advocates as judges for the Punjab and Haryana High Court. The collegium also approved three judicial officers for the Andhra Pradesh High Court. These appointments mark a significant development in the judiciary. The names of the appointed individuals have been released.

articles.title: Congress Dumps DMK, Backs Vijay's TVK In Major Tamil Nadu Shift
articles.description: Congress has left its alliance with the DMK in Tamil Nadu and decided to support Vijay’s TVK, which emerged as the largest party but lacks a majority. With five MLAs, Congress will help TVK form the government, but only if it keeps “communal forces” out. The alliance, called “Mission 2029”, aims to continue long-term.

articles.title: Vietnam President To Lam accorded ceremonial welcome at Rashtrapati Bhavan
articles.description: New Delhi: Vietnamese President To Lam was accorded a ceremonial welcome at the forecourt of Rashtrapati Bhavan on Wednesday. The visiting leader was warmly received by President Droupadi Murmu and Prime Minister Narendra Modi. Children dressed in traditional attire, holding the Vietnamese national flag alongside the Indian tricolour, also greeted him at the ceremony. President [...]

articles.title: Tamil Nadu Polls Are Over, But Real Suspense Begins — Will Crores Of Women Get Rs 1,000 This Month?
articles.description: Vijay won. Stalin resigned. A new Tamil Nadu is taking shape. Yet for crores of women, no election result matters more than one thing — their monthly Rs 1,000.

articles.title: 'Ball without Billionaires': Amazon union leader arrested after show to protest Bezos's Met Gala
articles.description: Organised by labour unions including the Service Employees International Union and the Amazon Labour Union, the 'Ball without Billionaires' show flipped the Met Gala’s theme of 'Fashion is Art' into a protest message: 'Labour is Art'.
